title: Total Taxable Resources
summary: This Treasury webpage describes Total Taxable Resources (TTR), a Congressionally mandated annual estimate of states' relative fiscal capacity used to allocate Community Mental Health Service and Substance Abuse Prevention and Treatment block grants. The source text contains no actual TTR data, only methodology references and links to historical PDF estimates (2018-2024).
key_points: TTR is required under Public Law 102-321 and developed from a 1986 Congressionally mandated study on Federal-State-Local fiscal relations
TTR estimates are used in formulas allocating Federal funds for SAMHSA-administered block grant programs
The source provides methodology working papers and links to annual TTR estimates from 1999-2024
No specific TTR numbers, state-level data, or fiscal capacity figures are present in the source text
The page serves as a navigation/index page to historical data files, not the data itself
The most recent estimates referenced are dated 09/30/2024
why_it_matters: This source is not directly relevant to market structure or macro catalysts as typically understood in financial research. TTR is an internal federal block grant allocation methodology; it does not represent fiscal policy decisions, economic data releases, or market-moving information. The page contains no actionable market intelligence.

issues_found: Source is a navigation/index page listing historical data files, not an article with substantive content (Rule 5 violation)
Source is not relevant to market structure or macro catalysts - TTR is an internal block grant allocation methodology with no market-moving information
